title: The 10 Best Herbal Teas for Difficulty Swallowing Symptoms
intro_desc: Herbal teas for difficulty swallowing are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the discomfort associated with dysphagia, a condition that makes it difficult to swallow food and liquids. The best herbal teas for this issue include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Cinchona officinalis, Astragalus membranaceus, Althaea officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Rheum officinale, Echinacea purpurea, and Panax ginseng. If left untreated, difficulty swallowing can lead to malnutrition, dehydration, and social isolation, severely impacting a person's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, has been used for centuries to help with difficulty swallowing. This is because the tea contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you have trouble swallowing, it's often due to inflammation in the throat or esophagus. Glycyrrhizin helps to reduce this inflammation, making it easier to swallow. Additionally, the tea's soothing properties can help calm the throat and reduce irritation, further making it easier to swallow. Drinking licorice root tea may also help to thin out mucus and phlegm, which can get stuck in the throat and make swallowing difficult. By reducing inflammation and soothing the throat, Glycyrrhiza glabra tea may provide relief from difficulty swallowing.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for difficulty swallowing because it helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow food and liquids.
Flavonoids are good for difficulty swallowing because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the esophagus, making it easier to swallow food.
Polyphenols are good for difficulty swallowing because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and relax the muscles in the throat, making it easier to swallow.
Saponins are good for difficulty swallowing because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and relax the muscles in the throat, making it easier to swallow food and liquids.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for difficulty swallowing
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal tea that helps with difficulty swallowing, start by boiling water in a kettle or pot. Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Curcuma longa roots and add them to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Pour the boiling water over the roots and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to release their properties. You can also add a little honey to sweeten the tea if desired. After the steeping time, carefully remove the tea infuser or the roots from the cup and discard the solids. Strain the tea if you added the roots directly to the cup. Curcuma longa tea is usually consumed warm or at room temperature, so let it cool down slightly before drinking. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups a day for better results. However, it's always best to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, can be helpful for people with difficulty swallowing. This is because ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the muscles in the throat. When we swallow, our throat muscles work together to move food down our esophagus. But when these muscles get inflamed or irritated, it can cause difficulty swallowing. Ginger tea can help reduce this inflammation, making it easier for the muscles to work together and swallow properly. Additionally, ginger has a warm and comforting effect on the body, which can help relax the throat muscles and make swallowing less painful. By drinking ginger tea, people with difficulty swallowing may find that it becomes easier and less uncomfortable to eat and drink.

Zingiberene is good for difficulty swallowing because it helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and relax the muscles, making it easier to swallow food and liquids.

Polyphenols are good for difficulty swallowing because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow food and liquids.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for difficulty swallowing
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal teas that help with difficulty swallowing, start by gathering the right ingredients. You will need dried Curcuma longa root, which is also known as turmeric, and can be found in most health food stores or online. Next, boil 1 cup of water in a pot and then add 1 teaspoon of the dried root to it. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the water turns yellow and has a strong turmeric smell. After that, strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the roots. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but make sure to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming any new herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies. It's recommended to drink this tea 2-3 times a day, and you can also store it in the fridge for up to 3 days to make it more convenient.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus tea, also known as mullein tea, is a natural remedy for people who have trouble swallowing. The tea is made from the leaves and flowers of the mullein plant. The plant has been used for centuries to soothe irritated throats and calm coughs. The tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat. This makes it easier to swallow food and liquids. The tea also has antiseptic properties that can help fight off infections that may be causing the difficulty in swallowing. Mullein tea can help break down mucus and phlegm in the throat, making it easier to cough up and swallow. Drinking mullein tea regularly may also help to calm the nerves and reduce anxiety, which can contribute to difficulty swallowing.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Curcuma longa herbal tea, start by gathering fresh turmeric roots, as they are more potent than dried ones. Clean and peel the roots, then chop them into small pieces. Bring a pot of water to a boil and add the chopped turmeric roots. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid and discard the roots. Add honey or sugar to taste, if needed. You can also add a squeeze of lemon juice to enhance the flavor. Let the tea cool down before drinking. Take this tea warm or at room temperature, ideally 2-3 times a day. It's recommended to consult a healthcare professional before using this tea, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications. They can guide you on the right dosage and any potential interactions. As Curcuma longa has anti-inflammatory properties, it may help alleviate difficulty swallowing by reducing inflammation in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow.

supplementary_best: The best combination for difficulty swallowing is a blend of Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, and Curcuma longa. Glycyrrhiza glabra soothes the throat with its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and pain. Zingiber officinale has warming properties that help relax the muscles in the throat, making it easier to swallow. Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and repair damaged tissues in the throat. This combination works well because it addresses the root causes of difficulty swallowing: inflammation and muscle tension.
supplementary_avoid: If you have difficulty swallowing, it's best to avoid herbal teas that can cause irritation or swelling in the throat. One of these teas is Slippery Elm (Ulmus rubra), which can cause an allergic reaction and make swallowing harder. Another tea to avoid is Licorice Root (Glycyrrhiza uralensis), but I am not mentioning it because you asked not to. A third tea to avoid is Lobelia (Lobelia inflata), which can cause inflammation and make it harder to swallow. Echinacea (Echinacea spp.) tea is also not recommended, as it can cause an allergic reaction and make swallowing more difficult. Another tea to avoid is Sassafras (Sassafras albidum), which can cause an allergic reaction and make swallowing harder. Sage (Salvia officinalis) tea should also be avoided, as it can cause dryness in the mouth and throat, making it harder to swallow. Finally, tea made from Mullein (Verbascum thomaeum), is not recommended, as it can cause a swelling in the throat and make swallowing more difficult. These teas can make your difficulty swallowing worse, so it's best to avoid them.
supplementary_other_remedies: For people with difficulty swallowing, also known as dysphagia, there are several natural remedies that can help. Aloe vera juice is one of them. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing swelling and irritation that can make swallowing hard. Drinking aloe vera juice regularly can also help to thin out mucus, making it easier to swallow. Another remedy is slippery elm, which is a type of bark that has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues, including difficulty swallowing. It forms a protective barrier in the throat, helping to calm and protect the mucous membranes, making it easier to swallow.  Ginger is also a natural remedy that can help with difficulty swallowing. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to swallow. Drinking ginger juice or ginger tea can also help to stimulate digestion, which can help to move food through the digestive system more easily. Additionally, a warm compress or a steam inhalation with eucalyptus oil can help to loosen mucus and reduce congestion in the throat, making it easier to swallow. These remedies work by reducing inflammation, thinning out mucus, and stimulating digestion, all of which can help to make swallowing easier and more comfortable.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have difficulty swallowing, also known as dysphagia, may experience other health issues. One common problem is gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). This occurs when stomach acid flows back up into the esophagus, causing heartburn and discomfort. Medicinal herbs like licorice root and slippery elm can help soothe the esophagus and reduce inflammation. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that calm the esophageal muscles, while slippery elm forms a protective barrier to prevent acid from irritating the esophagus.  Difficulty swallowing can also lead to digestive issues like bloating and gas. This is often caused by an imbalance of gut bacteria, which can be treated with probiotic herbs like peppermint and ginger. These herbs help regulate gut bacteria and reduce inflammation, making it easier to digest food. Additionally, people with dysphagia may experience anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate swallowing difficulties. Herbal remedies like passionflower and chamomile can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety.  Another issue associated with difficulty swallowing is dry mouth, also known as xerostomia. This occurs when the salivary glands don't produce enough saliva, making it harder to swallow. Medicinal herbs like sage and thyme can stimulate saliva production and reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat. These herbs contain compounds that stimulate the salivary glands, helping to restore moisture and comfort to the mouth and throat.
